salmanshh_dragndrop.name: DragNDrop
salmanshh_dragndrop.description: drop-in replacement for Construct 3's built-in Drag & Drop behaviour, designed for developers who need full control over how, when, and by what an object is grabbed and released. Where the built-in behaviour is permanently wired to the mouse or touch, 'DragNDrop' decouples every stage of the drag lifecycle from the input device. Grab and release can be triggered entirely through events

CancelGrab.description: Ends the current drag without computing throw velocity.

ClearThrowVelocity.description: Removes any manual throw velocity override.

ForceGrab.description: Cancels any current drag and starts a new one immediately.

anchorX.name: Anchor X
anchorX.desc: World-space X position of the anchor.

anchorY.name: Anchor Y
anchorY.desc: World-space Y position of the anchor.

grabMode.name: Grab mode
grabMode.desc: Keep the existing offset or snap to the anchor center.

Grab.description: Starts a drag using the provided anchor and grab mode.

anchorX.name: Anchor X
anchorX.desc: World-space X position of the anchor.

anchorY.name: Anchor Y
anchorY.desc: World-space Y position of the anchor.

grabMode.name: Grab mode
grabMode.desc: Keep the existing offset or snap to the anchor center.

Release.description: Ends the current drag and finalizes throw velocity.

SetAnchor.description: Updates the world-space anchor that the dragged object follows.

SetAnchorToObject.description: Uses the center of the selected object as the anchor point.

object.name: Object
object.desc: Object instance to use as the anchor source.

SetAxisLock.description: Constrains the dragged object to a single axis.

SetDistanceClamp.description: Limits how far the object can travel from its grab origin.

radius.name: Radius
radius.desc: Maximum travel distance in pixels. 0 disables the clamp.

SetEnabled.description: Enables or disables the drag behavior.

enabled.name: Enabled
enabled.desc: Whether the behavior should be enabled.

SetFollowSpeed.description: Sets the lerp speed for following the anchor.

speed.name: Speed
speed.desc: Pixels per second. 0 means instant snap.

SetGrabOffset.description: Overrides the offset used while the object is held.

SetThrowVelocity.description: Overrides the velocity that is reported on release.

velX.name: Velocity X
velX.desc: X component of the throw velocity.

velY.name: Velocity Y
velY.desc: Y component of the throw velocity.

IsAtDistanceLimit.description: True when the held object is at or beyond the clamp radius.

IsEnabled.description: True while the behavior is active.

IsHeld.description: True while the object is currently being dragged.

OnDistanceLimitReached.description: Triggered when the held object hits its clamp limit.

OnGrabCancelled.description: Triggered when drag is cancelled without release.

OnReleased.description: Triggered when the current drag ends.

WasThrown.description: True when the last release produced non-zero throw velocity.

AnchorX.description: Current world-space X of the anchor point.

AnchorY.description: Current world-space Y of the anchor point.

ClampRadius.description: Current distance clamp radius, or 0 if unconstrained.

DistanceFromOrigin.description: Distance from the grab origin to the object's current position.

GrabOffsetX.description: Current X component of the grab offset vector.

GrabOffsetY.description: Current Y component of the grab offset vector.

GrabOriginX.description: World-space X where the object was when grabbing started.

GrabOriginY.description: World-space Y where the object was when grabbing started.

IsHeldValue.description: Returns 1 when the object is currently held, otherwise 0.

ThrowSpeed.description: Magnitude of the final release velocity.

ThrowVelX.description: X component of the final release velocity.

ThrowVelY.description: Y component of the final release velocity.
